touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn message sent to Elijah Schneider introducing AskElephant's forecasting capabilities for sales teams. No response received.
Cold email sent highlighting how AskElephant helps managed IT/cybersecurity firms improve forecast accuracy. No response received.

Initial cold call with Elijah Schneider. He's recently joined as VP of Sales and is evaluating conversation intelligence tools. Expressed interest in improving forecasting accuracy across the sales team. Currently looking at Chorus as well. Brief intro call, agreed to schedule qualification discussion.

questions_asked.question: What's driving your interest in conversation intelligence right now?
questions_asked.answer: I just started as VP of Sales three months ago and our forecasting is all over the place. Need better visibility into what's actually happening in deals.

questions_asked.question: How does AskElephant differ from Chorus?
questions_asked.answer: We focus heavily on deal risk signals and forecasting accuracy with ML-driven insights, while Chorus is more coaching-focused. Happy to show you specifics in a deeper call.

next_steps: Schedule qualification call for next week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with calendar link for qualification call. No response needed.
Attempted to confirm qualification call time. Reached voicemail, left brief message.
Reminder email sent one day before scheduled qualification call with agenda items.
Pre-call email with discovery questions to help Elijah prepare for the qualification discussion.

Qualification call with Elijah. Team of 6 AEs struggling with forecast accuracy. He owns forecasting but hasn't brought in leadership yet. Interested in automations plus consulting package. Timeline is flexible, budget around $5-6K range. Also evaluating Chorus but concerned about their pricing model.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this decision?
questions_asked.answer: Right now it's mainly me. I'll probably loop in our CFO closer to signature, but I can move this forward on my own.

questions_asked.question: What's your current process for tracking deal health?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, it's mostly gut feel and weekly pipeline reviews. We don't have systematic capture of calls or emails.

questions_asked.question: Can you work with our existing CRM setup?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we integrate with all major CRMs including HubSpot and Salesforce.

objections: Need to make sure this doesn't add administrative burden to the AE team
next_steps: Send over case study from similar managed services company
Schedule discovery call to walk through current workflow

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call diving into CedarSpark's sales process. Elijah walked through typical deal cycle and pain points with forecasting. He's concerned about getting accurate deal health signals early. Discussed Chorus pricing concerns—they quoted higher. Wants to see how our automations package could reduce manual tracking work.

questions_asked.question: What does a win look like for you in 6 months?
questions_asked.answer: Being able to call my forecast within 10% accuracy and having data to back up my pipeline reviews instead of just rep opinions.

questions_asked.question: What kind of consulting is included in the package?
questions_asked.answer: We provide quarterly business reviews, custom report building, and ongoing optimization recommendations based on your team's patterns.

questions_asked.question: How long does implementation usually take?
questions_asked.answer: Typically 2-3 weeks for full team onboarding with our automations plus consulting tier.

next_steps: Elijah will prepare list of key workflows to automate

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation and full platform demo with Elijah. Walked through HNSW-based deal matching, XGBoost risk scoring, and automated call summarization. He was particularly interested in forecasting accuracy features and deal risk signals. Asked detailed questions about data privacy and integration requirements. Mentioned Chorus demo felt more generic. Discussed pricing—he's hoping to stay closer to $4-5K for 6 seats given budget constraints this quarter.

questions_asked.question: Where is our conversation data stored and who has access?
questions_asked.answer: All data is encrypted at rest in SOC 2 compliant infrastructure. Only your authorized team members have access, we never use your data to train models for other customers.

questions_asked.question: Can we start with fewer seats and scale up?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely, though our pricing model offers better per-seat rates at 6+ seats. You could start with 3-4 if you prefer.

questions_asked.question: What's your decision timeline looking like?
questions_asked.answer: Would like to have something in place by end of next month. Budget year resets soon so timing is good.

questions_asked.question: How does the consulting component actually work day-to-day?
questions_asked.answer: We assign a dedicated customer success manager who meets with you monthly, plus async support for report requests and optimization questions.

objections: Pricing is higher than I was hoping—need to stay within budget constraints
next_steps: Send formal proposal for 6 seats with automations plus consulting
Include ROI calculator based on their team size and deal volume

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call with Elijah. Presented pricing at $6,540 annually for 6 seats with automations plus consulting package. He pushed back on price, mentioned Chorus came in slightly lower and he's working with limited budget. Discussed value of consulting component vs. self-service. He requested discount to get closer to $4,500 range to make it work this quarter.

questions_asked.question: Is there any flexibility on the pricing given we're coming in at end of your quarter?
questions_asked.answer: Let me talk to my manager about what we can do. The consulting component is where most of the value lives, so there's a floor to how low we can go.

questions_asked.question: If we could get to a number that works, how quickly could you move forward?
questions_asked.answer: I can sign within a week once we agree on terms. I have authority up to $7K so this is within my range.

objections: Chorus quoted me about $5,200 for similar seat count
Need to justify the premium over alternatives to stay in budget
next_steps: Elijah will confirm final seat count and timeline internally

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with revised proposal at $4,400 ACV (32.7% discount) for 6 seats, automations plus consulting package. Elijah responded positively, said the pricing works and he'll review final terms with his team. Asked about contract start date flexibility. Indicated he's ready to move forward pending legal review.

questions_asked.question: Can we start the contract May 1st instead of immediately?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can set the start date for May 1st. Implementation can begin before then if you'd like to get set up early.

next_steps: Schedule kickoff call for late April
